rationale: Both evaluations award an identical 4/10 and surface the same substantive concerns — lack of SoC/IP modeling experience, no AI/deep-learning workload expertise, and missing hardware-software co-design. The only differences are rewordings (e.g., 'diverse programming experience' vs 'diverse programming language experience') and minor reframing of strengths around performance optimization, none of which key off the name change. This is ordinary run-to-run noise, not bias.
